assertions.assertion: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertions.evidence: investigation.md identifies Root Cause 1: 'Convention Reference Formatter Uses Wrong Case Transform' in shared/convention-utils.md (kebab-case instead of title case) and Root Cause 2: 'Task Creation Uses Feature Issue Type ID Instead of Task' in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a (reads 10142 instead of 10050).

assertions.assertion: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertions.evidence: investigation.md Independence Assessment states: 'Root Cause 1 is located in shared/convention-utils.md' and 'Root Cause 2 is located in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a'. States they are 'in different modules and different code paths' and 'This triggers the Decomposition Guard (Step 6)'.

assertions.assertion: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertions.evidence: decomposition-guard.md presents: '1. Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform (in shared/convention-utils.md)' and '2. Task creation uses Feature issue type ID instead of Task (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)'.

assertions.assertion: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertions.evidence: decomposition-guard.md presents '1. Proceed — create a single Task covering all fixes' and '2. Split — I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually' with 'Choose (1/2):'.

assertions.assertion: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertions.evidence: decomposition-guard.md explicitly states: 'Execution is paused. No Task will be created until the user selects an option. The skill will not silently bundle both fixes into a single Task.'
